# @zakkster/lite-headless / timeline

Vertical activity log with per-item markers. Each item has a `type`
(default/success/warning/danger/info) controlling marker color. Mostly
a layout + ARIA primitive — items are static and consumer-rendered;
the primitive paints data attributes for styling and ARIA roles for
screen-reader navigation.

## Quick example

```js
import { createTimeline } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/timeline";

const tl = createTimeline({});
tl.attachRoot(rootEl);
tl.attachItem(item1, { type: "success" });
tl.attachItem(item2, { type: "warning" });
tl.attachItem(item3, { type: "default" });
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| (none) | - | Timeline is a layout primitive; items carry the state |

## Item options (per `attachItem`)

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `type` | `"default"` | One of: `default`, `success`, `warning`, `danger`, `info` |

## API

```ts
{
    itemCount: number    // readonly

    attachRoot(el): off
    attachItem(el, opts?: { type?: string }): off
    setItemType(el, type: string): void   // mutate type after attach

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

### Root (`[data-timeline-root]`)

- `role="list"` (only if not pre-set)

### Items (`[data-timeline-item]`)

- `role="listitem"` (only if not pre-set)
- `data-type="default|success|warning|danger|info"`

### Markers (`[data-timeline-marker]` inside an item)

- `aria-hidden="true"` (decoration only — the item itself announces it)

## Wrapper (`<lite-timeline>`)

```html
<lite-timeline>
    <div data-timeline-item data-type="success">
        <span data-timeline-marker></span>
        <time data-timeline-time>09:14</time>
        <div data-timeline-content>
            <strong>Order #1234</strong> shipped to customer
        </div>
    </div>
    <div data-timeline-item data-type="warning">
        <span data-timeline-marker></span>
        <time data-timeline-time>08:52</time>
        <div data-timeline-content>
            Inventory low for SKU-887
        </div>
    </div>
    <div data-timeline-item>
        <span data-timeline-marker></span>
        <time data-timeline-time>08:30</time>
        <div data-timeline-content>
            <strong>alice</strong> logged in
        </div>
    </div>
</lite-timeline>
```

Items are auto-wired via the role observer; you can dynamically add
or remove `<div data-timeline-item>` children and they'll be attached
automatically.

Host methods: `setItemType(el, type)`.

## CSS contract

`data-type` is a Class-3 enum. Consumer styles the marker color per
type via `[data-timeline-marker]` selectors inside `[data-type="..."]`.
Example:

```css
[data-timeline-item] {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 16px 80px 1fr;
    gap: 12px;
    padding: 8px 0;
    position: relative;
}
[data-timeline-marker] {
    width: 10px; height: 10px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    background: var(--accent);
    margin-top: 4px;
}
[data-timeline-item][data-type="success"] [data-timeline-marker] { background: var(--green); }
[data-timeline-item][data-type="warning"] [data-timeline-marker] { background: var(--orange); }
[data-timeline-item][data-type="danger"]  [data-timeline-marker] { background: var(--red); }
```

For the connecting line between markers, use a `::before` pseudo-element
on the item (or absolute-positioned line on the root).
